name: Pydantic v2
what_it_does: Data validation and settings management library for Python using type annotations. Pydantic v2 rewrote the core in Rust for 5-50x performance improvement. Validates incoming data (API requests, config files, environment variables) against typed Python models. The standard data modeling library for FastAPI — every FastAPI request/response model uses Pydantic.
use_cases: Define typed data models for LLM API request/response parsing with automatic validation and JSON schema generation
Validate and parse agent tool call arguments against typed schemas with descriptive error messages
Load and validate agent configuration from environment variables and config files with BaseSettings
Generate JSON schemas from Python type annotations for OpenAI function calling tool definitions
Serialize agent data models to/from JSON for API responses with field aliasing and custom serializers
not_for: Runtime type enforcement beyond data boundaries — Pydantic validates at object creation; Python remains dynamically typed internally
Complex business rule validation — Pydantic handles structural/type validation; use separate domain logic for business rules
Non-Python codebases — Pydantic is Python-specific; JSON Schema validators exist for other languages
best_when: You're building a Python API or agent with FastAPI and need typed, validated data models with automatic JSON schema generation and error messaging.
avoid_when: You just need simple dataclasses without validation — Python's built-in dataclasses are lighter; Pydantic adds value when validation and serialization are needed.

auth.notes: Local validation library — no authentication required.

pricing.paid_tiers: None (Pydantic Logfire is a separate observability product)
pricing.notes: MIT license. Core library free. Pydantic Logfire (observability platform) is a separate commercial product.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. v2 Rust core is 5-50x faster than v1 Python implementation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ValidationError includes all validation failures in a structured list with field paths, input values, and error types. Human-readable str(error) and machine-readable error.errors() are both available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Validation is pure — same input always produces same validation result or same error.
known_agent_gotchas: Pydantic v2 has breaking changes from v1 — __validators__ replaced by @field_validator, orm_mode renamed to from_attributes, .dict() renamed to .model_dump(); v1 migration requires code changes
Field aliases (alias='camelCase') affect both serialization and deserialization — use model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True) to accept both the alias and field name
Computed fields (@computed_field) are included in serialization but can't be set on model creation — they're derived from other fields; attempting to set them raises ValueError
model.model_dump(mode='json') serializes types like datetime, UUID, and Decimal to JSON-compatible primitives — plain model_dump() returns Python objects which aren't JSON-serializable
Optional[T] is not the same as T | None in Pydantic v2 — both work but their validation behavior differs for required vs. optional fields; prefer T | None with explicit default=None
Recursive/self-referential models require model_rebuild() call after class definition — Pydantic can't resolve forward references automatically for circular schemas

security_score_components.security_notes: Validation prevents malformed data from entering agent logic. Use SecretStr type for sensitive fields — prevents accidental secret exposure in logs and repr(). Rust core reduces risk of Python memory safety issues.
